notes: SABSA is a risk- and business-driven enterprise security architecture methodology developed by John Sherwood et al. and maintained by the SABSA Institute. Frequently cited alongside TOGAF in security-architecture work. Materials and certifications are paywalled / training-gated; the methodology body of knowledge is not publicly redistributable.

data.notes: Paywalled / training-gated. Six-layered matrix model spanning Contextual, Conceptual, Logical, Physical, Component, and Operational architecture views. The 'SABSA Blue Book' (Enterprise Security Architecture: A Business-Driven Approach) is the canonical reference text.
